description: A template proved once scales to a theorem proved forever.

hints.text: The goal is $\lnot \lnot (A \land B) \to (A \land B)$ — [double-negation|double negation] elimination applied to a [conjunction|conjunction]. You could prove this from scratch with `not-intro` gymnastics. Or you could prove the [atom|atomic] version $\lnot \lnot P \to P$ and substitute $P$ with $(A \land B)$. The `subst` rule turns a 2-step proof into a 3-step one. First, assume $\lnot \lnot P$.
Substitute $P$ with $(A \land B)$. The engine replaces every occurrence of $P$ in both the [context|context] and [conclusion|conclusion] of step 3.
